relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file provides account balance data from the accounts department to the credit department, and the outbound file transmits credit score assessment results from credit to risk_and_compliance. The credit department plausibly uses account balance information (customer_id, account details, balances) as input to generate credit assessments. Both files share customer_id as a common identifier and follow a logical data pipeline where credit decisions depend on account data.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent identifier exists in inbound schema; likely system-generated by credit department.
Both are ISO8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or influenced by inbound message timestamp, though may be regenerated at assessment time.
Inbound recipient is 'credit_department'; outbound source_department is 'credit', representing the same logical department in different notation.
No inbound field specifies the downstream destination; this is a routing decision made by the credit system.
Exact field name and meaning; customer_id from inbound account data maps directly to outbound customer identification.
Inbound schema contains no customer name field; this information is not provided in account_balance.json.
Inbound schema contains no date_of_birth field; this personal demographic data is not in account_balance.json.
Inbound account_id could represent the account number, though format differs (account_id vs IBAN); mapping is plausible but uncertain.
No numerical credit score exists in inbound schema; this is computed by the credit department's scoring algorithm.
No score range metadata exists in inbound schema; this is a system constant or configuration.
No score range metadata exists in inbound schema; this is a system constant or configuration.
No categorical rating exists in inbound schema; this is derived from the credit scoring algorithm.
Assessment date likely corresponds to when the inbound message was received, though may be independently set by credit system.
No credit limit information exists in inbound account balance schema.
Inbound balance (especially for credit_card accounts where negative indicates amount owed) relates to outstanding balance concept.
Utilization rate could be computed from balance and available_balance, though the exact calculation is not specified in inbound schema.
No payment history data exists in inbound account balance snapshot; this requires historical transaction data not provided.
No delinquency information exists in inbound schema; this requires historical payment performance data.
No risk assessment data exists in inbound schema; this is computed by credit risk models.
No credit decision recommendation exists in inbound schema; this is determined by credit policy and scoring.
No fraud risk assessment exists in inbound account balance schema; this requires separate fraud detection systems.

No regulatory concern data exists in inbound account balance schema; this requires separate regulatory screening.
No workflow status exists in inbound schema; this is managed by the credit assessment workflow system.

No signature or audit trail exists in inbound schema; this is generated for compliance and audit purposes.
